text: Heavy-load eccentric calf muscle training for 12 weeks restored full running activity in all 15 recreational athletes with chronic Achilles tendinosis who had failed conventional treatment.
population: recreational athletes with chronic Achilles tendinosis, mean age 44

text: Eccentric calf muscle training significantly decreased pain during activity and restored calf muscle strength to match the noninjured side in chronic Achilles tendinosis patients.
population: recreational athletes with chronic Achilles tendinosis, mean age 44

text: Conventional treatment (rest, NSAIDs, orthotics, physical therapy, and ordinary training programs) failed in all 15 comparison patients with chronic Achilles tendinosis, all of whom ultimately required surgery.
population: recreational athletes with chronic Achilles tendinosis

text: Chronic Achilles tendinosis is associated with significantly lower eccentric and concentric calf muscle strength on the injured side compared with the noninjured side.
population: recreational athletes with chronic Achilles tendinosis

text: Heavy-load eccentric calf muscle training is an effective nonsurgical alternative for chronic Achilles tendinosis that has not responded to conventional conservative treatment.
population: recreational athletes in their early forties
